items.title: Sri Lanka’s Bumpy Road to a Political Reset
items.summary: Twin elections in 2024 transformed Sri Lanka’s political landscape, bringing to power a president and parliamentarians who pledged sweeping reforms. So far, however, the new government has made little progress toward this end. It has work to do to show it can do politics differently.

items.title: ‘CanisterWorm’ Springs Wiper Attack Targeting Iran
items.summary: A financially motivated data theft and extortion group is attempting to inject itself into the Iran war, unleashing a worm that spreads through poorly secured cloud services and wipes data on infected systems that use Iran's time zone or have Farsi set as the default language.

items.title: Health & Human Services warning NC about Legionnaires' disease after increase in cases - WRAL
items.summary: Legionnaires' disease is a severe form of pneumonia caused by the bacterium known as Legionella. According to Mayo Clinic, most people who catch Legionnaires' disease breathe in the bacteria from water or soil.
